questions.question: What conventional wisdom did Emu3 attack head-on?
options: That transformers can't process video

That image generation needs diffusion — the claim was that discrete tokens lose too much detail and autoregressive sampling accumulates error, which is why SD, DALL-E 3, Imagen, and Midjourney all used diffusion
That bigger models are always better
That CLIP loss is mandatory for VLMs
questions.explanation: Emu3's bet: better visual tokenizer + enough scale + plain next-token loss = diffusion-beating generation in the same model that also does perception. A single Llama-style decoder beat SDXL on generation and LLaVA-1.6 on perception.

questions.question: What does Emu3's image tokenizer produce, and why is tokenizer quality "the ceiling"?
options: 16 tokens per image at K=256

A 512x512 image becomes 64x64 = 4096 tokens at codebook size 32768 (8x8 reduction per token), with reconstruction PSNR of 30.5 dB — close to Stable Diffusion's 32 dB continuous latents; the model can never generate detail the tokenizer can't reconstruct
Continuous embeddings, not discrete tokens
One token per object detected
questions.explanation: Emu3 trains a custom IBQ-class tokenizer (SBER-MoVQGAN family). Part of the paper's contribution is simply "we trained a very good tokenizer" — that closes most of the assumed quality gap with diffusion.

questions.question: How does the 3D video tokenizer work, and how many tokens does a 4s clip take?
options: Each frame is tokenized independently at full resolution
A spatiotemporal 4x4x4-pixel patch maps to one integer — a 4s/8FPS clip at 256x256 with 4x spatial and 4x temporal reduction yields 64 × 64 × 8 = 32,768 tokens
Audio and video share one token stream
Only keyframes are tokenized
questions.explanation: Patches span time, so the codebook captures motion, not just appearance. 32 frames compress 4x temporally to 8 token-frames of 64x64 spatial tokens each.

questions.question: What inference trick borrowed from diffusion does Emu3 rely on for image quality?
options: A denoising schedule over 50 steps
Classifier-free guidance — generate logits twice (full caption vs empty caption) and mix them with a guidance weight of roughly 3.0-7.0; temperature 0.8 for generation, 1.0 for perception
Beam search with width 100
Rejection sampling against CLIP score
questions.explanation: The training objective is pure next-token prediction with teacher forcing; CFG is an inference-time quality lever. Temperature too high gives artifacts, too low gives mode collapse.

questions.question: How does Emu3 learn WHEN to emit image vs text tokens with only one loss?
options: A router network switches modalities

From the data distribution — training mixes formats like "<caption> <image> tokens </image>" for generation and "<image> tokens </image> <question> text" for perception, with modality-specific loss weights but an identical loss function
A hard-coded rule fires after 77 tokens
Separate decoder heads per modality
questions.explanation: Generation emerges from predicting image tokens after the <image> tag. One weight set serves three roles: Emu3-Gen (image gen), Emu3-Chat (perception), Emu3-Stage2 (video gen).
